titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/237

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=202 status=live modified=2017-03-14 18:43:21.995354+00 | translation moa=203 status=live modified=2017-03-14 18:43:24.496271+00

div.p:
.V. dei gratia Rex Scott’. Omnibus probis hominibus totius terre sue salutem. Sciant presentes ⁊ posteri me dedisse
⁊ concessisse . ⁊ hac mea carta confirmasse . deo ⁊ ecclesie sancte crucis de edenburc . ⁊ canonicis ibidem
deo seruientibus . in exscambio elemosinarum quas per castellanos amiserunt  iiii. marcas de molendino meo de
Karramunt . ⁊ .xxxiii. solidos de molendino de Libertun’ . ⁊ de firma de Merihennest’ .xx. solidos . ⁊ de terra
furbarii .viii. solidos . ⁊ de firma clerenbaud .v. solidos . ⁊ .viii. denarios . Tenendum sibi in liberam ⁊ quietam elemosinam
ita libere ⁊ quiete . sicut alias elemosinas suas liberius ⁊ quietius tenent ⁊ possident . donec predictas elemosinas
quas per castellanos de Edenburc amiserunt  eis warrantizare potero . uel alibi redditum .ix. marcarum assigna
uero. Testibus Hug’ Episcopo Sancti Andree . Waltero olifart Iusticia . Hug’ Giffart . Waltero de Berchelei . Willelmo de Haia .
Roberto de Berchelei. Apud Adingtun’.

William by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, Greeting: Let present and future know that I have given and granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there, in exchange for the alms that they lost through the garrison, 4 merks from my mill of Cramond, and 33 shillings from the mill of Liberton, and from the ferm of Merchiston 20 shillings, and from the land of the furbisher 8 shillings, and from the ferm of Clermiston 5 shillings and 8 pence. For them to hold in free and quiet alms as freely and quietly as they hold and possess their other alms freely and quietly, until I shall be able to warrant to them the said alms which they lost through the garrison of Edinburgh, or until I shall have assigned an income of 9 merks elsewhere. As witnesses, Hugh bishop of St Andrews, Walter Oliphant the justice, Hugh Gifford, Walter of Berkeley, William de la Haye, Robert of Berkeley. At Haddington.
